nyheter.summary:
Freire Shipyard in Vigo has launched the Spanish Navy’s new diving support vessel, the Armada Diving Support Vessel A22 Proserpina. The compact steel-hulled vessel is intended to support the Spanish Navy’s Military Diving School (Escuela Militar de Buceo – EMB) with day-to-day work that ranges from training support to inspections and technical interventions. nyheter.title: REGENT’s Squire Seaglider Drone Performs Ground-effect Flight
nyheter.summary:
REGENT announced the successful first ground-effect flight of Squire, its autonomous Seaglider drone built for defense missions. This milestone represents the first time a defense-specific wing-in-ground effect (WIG) craft has flown in the United States, positioning the U.S. to overtake China in this critical technology space.   REGENT press release The flight demonstration marks the latest ...

nyheter.summary:
The French Navy will scale down its participation in the upcoming iteration of the largest joint military exercises between the Philippines and the United States, citing a shift in deployment due to the Middle East crisis. France will send “between 15 and 20” troops instead of deploying about 150 personnel embarked on an amphibious assault ...

nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) is operating off the coast of Namibia, as it sails around the African continent and is set to join a growing naval force in the Arabian Sea amid a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, USNI News has learned. Bush, which deployed at the end of March, did not sail through the Strait of Gibraltar and into the Mediterranean Sea, a typical transit for East Coast-based carriers headed to the Middle East. nyheter.title: Navy Commissions USS Harvey C. Barnum Jr. into the Fleet
nyheter.summary: The Navy welcomed its newest destroyer USS Harvey C. Barnum Jr. (DDG-124) into the fleet Saturday during a ceremony in Norfolk, Va., which is among the last Flight IIA configurations of the Arleigh Burke-class. The destroyer was commissioned at Naval Station Norfolk before approximately 1,800 guests, including senior military leaders, sailors, Marines, veterans and family members. nyheter.title: ‘Integrity, Arriving’
nyheter.summary: NAVAL BASE SAN DIEGO, Calif. — A boom across the Pacific near San Diego late Friday afternoon signaled the Orion capsule’s return to Earth’s atmosphere. The shock wave also summoned sailors tasked with safely returning the four astronauts following their 10-day lunar flight. It was time to go to work. Seay (T-AKR 302) docked last month in the Port of Cagayan de Oro to conduct the first-ever offloading of American maritime prepositioning force equipment on the Southern Philippine Island of Mindanao. nyheter.title: Navy to Inactivate Attack Boat USS Boise After $1.6B Repair Effort
nyheter.summary: One of the youngest Los Angeles-class nuclear attack submarines will be inactivated after waiting more than a decade for an overhaul, the Navy announced on Friday. USS Boise (SSN-764), currently in a drydock at HII Newport News Shipbuilding, Va., has been scheduled for a regular overhaul since Fiscal Year 2016 but has spent years pierside waiting for repairs. nyheter.title: Navy to mothball USS Boise, capping off years of maintenance challenges
nyheter.summary: The Navy said the decision to mothball the Boise aligns with a broader, “data-driven initiative” that aims to enhance the composition of the Navy’s fleet.

nyheter.title: Vessels Trickle Past Iran as Questions Remain Over Strait of Hormuz Status
nyheter.summary: Traffic flow in the Strait of Hormuz will likely continue at a slow trickle despite a two-week ceasefire between Iran and the United States, maritime experts agree. The majority of transits continue to use the so-called Tehran Tollbooth, which requires permission from the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, despite claims from Trump administration officials that the passage is open. nyheter.title: South Korean Submarine Completes First Leg of Indo-Pacific Voyage to Canada
nyheter.summary: A Republic of Korea Navy attack boat docked in Guam on Tuesday, completing the first leg of what will be the longest voyage of a South Korean submarine to date. ROKS Dosan Ahn Chang-ho (SS-083), the first of Seoul’s KSS-III-class of diesel electric submarines, departed South Korea last month for Canada ahead of drills with the Royal Canadian Navy in support of Seoul’s pitch to Ottawa’s 12 attack boat program. rov_teknologi.title: 5 Killed in U.S. Strikes on Suspected Narco Boats in Eastern Pacific
rov_teknologi.summary: Forces with Joint Task Force Southern Spear struck two vessels in the Eastern Pacific on Saturday, the first such strikes in April. Two men were killed in the first strike while the second strike killed three men. One person survived the first strike, according to a late Sunday U.S. Southern Command release. rov_teknologi.title: Navy, Marine Corps See Drop in 2024 Suicide Rate, Overall Trend is Rising, New Data Says
rov_teknologi.summary: This story has been updated to correct who provided gunlocks. The Navy and the Marine Corps saw the lowest suicide rate in four years, according to the Pentagon’s recently released suicide report covering calendar year 2024. In 2024, the Navy had 60 suicides, while the Marine Corps had 46, according to the annual suicide report, which the Pentagon quietly released March 31. The Marine Corps saw a huge drop in its suicide rate, going from 37.1 per 100,000 in 2023 to 27.3 per 100,000 in 202

A Suffren-class submarine deployed the Razorback, launching and recovering the drone with its dry deck shelter, from March 16-20 while operating off the coast of Toulon, France. rov_teknologi.title: Seoul Expands Sub Warfare Force with U.S. Helos, Patrol Aircraft
rov_teknologi.summary: The South Korean Navy stood up its first American-made MH-60R Seahawk helicopters Wednesday in the service’s latest move to bolster Seoul’s submarine hunting forces. Ordered in a $878 million deal from 2020, 12 anti-submarine helicopters were procured under the Republic of Korea Navy’s (ROKN) Maritime Helicopter-II program.
